data.title: Viking Holdings Ltd Non-GAAP EPS of $1.20 beats by $0.01, revenue of $2B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Viking Holdings Ltd press release (VIK): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.20 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $2B (+19.0% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $703.5 million, an increase of $149.2 million, or 26.9%, over the same period in 2024.
  • Adjusted Gross Margin for the third quarter of 2025 was $1,333.7 million, an increase of $234.8 million, or 21.4%, over the same period in 2024. Net Yield was $617 for the third quarter of 2025, up 7.1% year-over year.
  • Net Leverage improved from 2.1x as of June 30, 2025 to 1.6x as of September 30, 2025.
  • As of November 2, 2025, for its Core Products, Viking had sold 96% of its Capacity Passenger Cruise Days for the 2025 season and 70% of its Capacity Passenger Cruise Days for the 2026 season.
  • Based on the committed orderbook, the company expects to take delivery of two river vessels during the remainder of 2025.

data.title: OpenAI and Target partner to bring new AI-powered experiences across retail
data.description:
• Target (TGT) has become the latest retailer to partner with OpenAI (OPENAI) as it looks to accelerate product discovery, streamline operations, and elevate the guest experience ahead of the holiday season.
  • With the Target app in ChatGPT, customers will be able to get personalized recommendations, add everything they need to a cart with multi-item baskets, and check out using Drive Up, Order Pickup, or shipping.
  • Under the partnership, Target (TGT) will continue using OpenAI APIs and ChatGPT Enterprise to boost employee productivity and enhance Target’s guest experiences.
  • The partnership, set to launch next week as a test, will enable customers to tag Target in ChatGPT and share what they're looking for.

data.title: EU court dismisses Amazon's request to remove EU tech tag
data.description:
The EU's General Court dismissed a request by Amazon (AMZN) to scrap its designation as a 'very large online platform' under the bloc's Digital Services Act.

"DSA: Amazon’s action against the Commission's decision designating the platform Amazon Store as a 'very large online platform' is dismissed," said the General Court.

The Court of Justice of the European Union, or CJEU, consists of two courts — the Court of Justice and the General Court.

The EU's DSA regulates online intermediaries and platforms, which millions of Europeans use every day. The DSA protects consumers and their rights online, and its main goal is to prevent illegal and harmful activities online and the spread of disinformation. Very large online platforms, or VLOPs, with more than 45 millionusers in the EU come under DSA. Certain services from companies like Apple (AAPL), Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) Meta Platforms (META), Amazon, Alibaba (BABA), Microsoft (MSFT), ByteDance's (BDNCE) TikTok and X, formerly Twitter, are subject to rules under the DSA.

Amazon had contested that the provision infringes several fundamental rights guaranteed by the Charter of Fundamental Rights of the EU, including the freedom to conduct a business, the right to property, the principle of equality before the law, freedom of expression and information, and the right to private life and the protection of confidential information.

The General Court said that "interference, which is provided for by law and does not affect the essence of the freedom to conduct a business, is justified for the purpose of the Charter of Fundamental Rights. The EU legislature, which has a broad discretion, did not commit a manifest error in considering that very large online platforms, including marketplaces exceeding the threshold of 45 million users, could pose systemic risks to society, in particular by disseminating illegal content or infringing fundamental rights, including consumer protection."

The court noted that the obligations imposed on those platforms, such as those relating to the option of a recommender system which is not based on profiling, the public repository of advertisements or researchers' access to certain data, are intended to prevent those risks, even if they "entail significant financial burdens for those platforms."

The court also dismissed all other arguments by the U.S. online retail and tech giant.

Amazon did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Amazon told Reuters that it was disappointed in the ruling and that it intended to launch an appeal.

"The Very Large Online Platform status was designed to address systemic risks posed by very large companies with advertising as their primary revenue and that distribute speech and information," said the company, the report added. "The Amazon Store, as an online marketplace, does not pose any such systemic risks; it only sells goods, and it doesn’t disseminate or amplify information, views or opinions."

data.title: Kingsoft Cloud GAAP EPS of $0.00 beats by $0.15, revenue of $348.1M beats by $0.43M
data.description:
• Kingsoft Cloud press release (KC): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.00 beats by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $348.1M (+31.4% Y/Y) beats by $0.43M.
  • Revenues from public cloud services were RMB1,752.3 million (US$246.1 million), significantly increased by 49.1% from RMB1,175.5 million in the same quarter of 2024 and increased by 7.8% from RMB1,625.3 million last quarter. 
  • Revenues from enterprise cloud services were RMB725.7 million (US$101.9 million), compared with RMB710.0 million in the same quarter of 2024 and RMB723.9 million last quarter.
  • Non-GAAP EBITDA [5] was RMB826.6 million (US$116.1 million), increased by 345.9% from RMB185.4 million in the same quarter of 2024 and 103.6% from RMB406.0 million last quarter. Non-GAAP EBITDA margin was 33.4%, compared with 9.8% in the same quarter of 2024 and 17.3% in the previous quarter.

data.title: Ukraine presses EU to decide on loan backed by frozen Russian assets next month
data.description:
Ukraine is pressing European allies to decide on a proposed ~$162B loan backed by frozen Russian assets next month, as Kyiv faces a major budget shortfall and a corruption scandal.

European Union leaders failed to agree on the loan last month due to concerns raised by Belgium. The leaders are expected to discuss the loan at a summit on Dec. 18.

"We don't expect all technical details to be finalized by that time, but the architecture for issuing the loan must be agreed," Iryna Mudra, top legal adviser in Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's administration, told Reuters.

Mudra added that it's important for Ukraine to be involved in the decision. "Without Ukraine's direct involvement, assistance risks becoming ineffective only because we know the real needs on the ground."

On Monday, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen pitched three options to financially support Ukraine. These are the loan backed by frozen Russian assets, grants provided by EU members and a loan funded by the bloc's borrowing on financial markets.

Ukraine's remaining needs for 2026-2027 amounted to €135.7B. The proposed loan backed by frozen Russian assets would amount to €140B.

Most of these assets are on the accounts of Belgian securities depository Euroclear. Belgium is concerned that it could be held liable if Russia legally challenges Euroclear.

Meanwhile, Ukraine has uncovered a $100M kickback scheme involving senior energy officials and the state nuclear power company. Two ministers have stepped down, and the opposition has called for the whole cabinet to be dismissed.

data.title: Bullish GAAP EPS of $0.10 misses by $1.49, revenue of $76.5M beats by $5.36M; gives Q4 guidance
data.description:
• Bullish press release (BLSH): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.10 misses by $1.49.
  • Adjusted Revenue of $76.5M (+71.5% Y/Y) beats by $5.36M.
  • Digital asset sales were $41.6 billion vs. $54.2 billion.
  • Adjusted transaction revenue (non-IFRS) was $26.7 million vs. $32.9 million
  • Adjusted net income (non-IFRS) was $13.8 million vs. $(3.1) million
  • Adjusted EBITDA (non-IFRS) was $28.6 million vs. $7.7 million.

  • Q4 2025 Guidance

    To give investors insight into our business and expectations, management is providing the following guidance for the fourth quarter 2025:

    • Subscription, Services & Other Revenue (non-IFRS) of $47.0 million to $53.0 million
    • Adjusted Operating Expenses (non-IFRS) of $48.0 million to $50.0 million

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Target beats Q3 profit estimates, but misses on revenue; narrows FY EPS outlook
Target slips as investors eye the profit guidance for the key holiday quarter
data.description:
Target Corporation (TGT) slipped in early trading as investors eyed the retailer's guidance for the crucial holiday quarter.

Sales fell 1.5% year-over-year to $25.3 billion in the quarter, reflecting a merchandise sales decrease of 1.9%, partially offset by a 17.7% increase in non-merchandise sales.  Comparable sales decreased 2.7% in the quarter to miss the consensus expectation for a decline of 2.1%. The total comparable sales decline reflected a comparable store sales decline of 3.8%, partially offset by comparable digital sales growth of 2.4%. 

By sales category, food & beverage and hardlines delivered comparable sales growth in the quarter, offset by continued softness across the broader discretionary portfolio.

Operating income was down 18.9% to $0.9 billion. Excluding non-recurring items, operating income was $1.1 billion. Target's (TGT) gross margin rate was down 10 basis points to 28.2%, reflecting merchandising pressure from increased markdowns, partially offset by growth in advertising and other revenues, lower inventory shrink, and efficiency gains in supply chain and digital fulfillment. 

The Minneapolis-based company repurchased $152 million of its shares in the quarter, retiring 1.7 million shares of common stock at an average price of $91.59. At the end of the quarter, Target (TGT) had approximately $8.3 billion of remaining capacity under the repurchase program approved by the board in August 2021.

For the trailing twelve months through the third quarter of 2025, after-tax return on invested capital (ROIC) was 13.4 percent, compared with 15.9 percent for the trailing twelve months through the third quarter of 2024. 

Looking ahead, Target (TGT) sees full-year adjusted EPS of about $7 to $8 (midpoint $7.50) vs. the prior outlook for $7 to $9 and consensus estimate of $7.24. "As we head into the all-important holiday season, our team is well-prepared and ready to serve our guests with the great products, value, and inspiration they expect from Target," updated CEO Michael Fiddelke.

Shares of Target (TGT) were down 1.4% in premarket action to $87.27 vs. the 52-week range of $85.36 to $145.08. Walmart (WMT) and Costco (COST) were both slightly higher in the early session.

data.title: Target in charts: Q3 comparable sales -2.7% as digital growth fails to fully offset decline in store sales
Stock index futures inch up as investors look to Nvidia earnings
data.description:
Stock index futures pushed slightly higher Wednesday after a tough start to the week for equities.

Benchmark S&P 500 futures (SPX) advanced about half a percent, and so did Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND). Dow futures (INDU) were marginally up.

Wall Street is coming off its first four-day losing streak since August, as the artificial intelligence trade keeps taking it on the chin. After two days of risk-off selling on AI valuation and spending concerns, investors will likely continue to be cautious until Nvidia (NVDA) reports after the bell. 

Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid highlighted that Nvidia has now fallen more than 12% from its October 29 peak — its sharpest drop since the “Liberation Day” volatility earlier this year. He noted that even a major new AI deal failed to lift sentiment: Nvidia committed up to $10B to Anthropic and Microsoft up to $5B, with Anthropic set to buy $30B in Azure compute, yet both stocks declined on the day. Reid said "sentiment has turned more negative in the last few weeks, with the circular AI deals being treated with increasing caution as the conversation around a potential bubble has gathered pace."
